fact: March 29 sits exactly 88 days into the year, which means there are exactly 277 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for maple trees to start dripping their sweet sap - some trees can produce up to 10 gallons of sap in a single day around now!
Why do so many baby animals seem extra bouncy right now? March 29 falls during peak spring energy season when longer daylight triggers hyperactive hormones in young mammals!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this date because we're still close to the spring equinox - your shadow at noon points true north!
March 29 marks the time when hibernating bears in cold regions are starting to wake up super cranky and hungry after months without food - they've lost up to 30% of their body weight!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date falls during autumn when spider activity peaks as they rush to find warm hiding spots before winter!
The zodiac sign switches from Pisces to Aries right around now, which ancient people believed made the air itself feel more energetic and fiery!
Earthworms go absolutely crazy around March 29 in temperate regions - the soil temperature hits their favorite range and they can move up to 27 feet in a single day!
This date falls during the peak time for avalanches in mountain regions because warming spring temperatures make snow layers super unstable and slidey!
March 29 happens during cherry blossom season in Japan, when some trees bloom so explosively they can go from bare branches to full pink fluff in just 3 days!
The daylight on this date lasts almost exactly 12 hours in most places, but every day after gets about 3 minutes longer in the north - that adds up fast!
Migrating birds flying north around this time can smell spring from up to 100 miles away thanks to the chemical scents released by warming soil and budding plants!
